rawOutputHead:
{
  "verdict": "needs-attention",
  "summary": "Cannot complete a defensible adversarial review with only metadata. The diff spans 84 files (+6,623/−480) including new retry/backoff infrastructure (retry.mjs, bigmodel-errors.mjs), a deleted module (process.mjs), and modified state/job-tracking — all high-risk surfaces. The commit history shows six rounds of post-review hotfixes (Bundles A–F+), which is a strong signal of residual fragility. Requesting file-level access before signing off.",
  "findings": [
    {
      "severity": "high",
      "title": "Deleted process.mjs — potential dangling import causing runtime crash",
      "body": "scripts/lib/process.mjs (67 lines) was removed entirely. scripts/check-imports.mjs was modified (+5/−1), suggesting import-path remediation, but the diff stat alone cannot confirm every consumer was updated. A single remaining `import … from './lib/process.mjs'` or `from './lib/process'` will throw ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND at runtime with no compile-time guard (ESM).",
      "file": "scripts/lib/process.mjs",
      "line_start": 1,
      "line_end": 67,
      "confidence": 0.6,
      "recommendation": "Run `grep -r 'process\\.mjs\\|/process[\"'\\''"]' scripts/` across the tree. If any hits remain outside process.mjs itself, the deletion is incomplete and will crash at import time."
    },
    {
      "severity": "high",
      "title": "New retry.mjs (203 lines) + bigmodel-errors.mjs (168 lines) — unreviewed retry/backoff infrastructure",
      "body": "Retry logic is a top-tier defect surface: wrong error classification causes silent infinite retry or premature hard-fail; missing idempotency guards cause duplicate side-effects on POST/PATCH; improper backoff can exhaust rate limits or stall pipelines. The commit log mentions 'auto retry/backoff' in e0a9ba8 but no file contents were provided to verify: (1) which HTTP methods are retried, (2) whether error classification is complete and tested for all documented BigModel error codes, (3) whether retries are bounded and jittered, (4) whether the retry path is idempotent for non-GET requests. 352 lines of retry tests exist (tests/retry.test.mjs) but coverage cannot be assessed from metadata.",
      "file": "scripts/lib/retry.mjs",
      "line_start": 1,
      "line_end": 203,
      "confidence": 0.55,
      "recommendation": "Provide retry.mjs, bigmodel-errors.mjs, and glm-client.mjs for line-level review. Specifically verify: (a) retry is limited to idempotent operations or guarded by idempotency keys, (b) error classification covers all BigModel error codes with tests for each, (c) max retry count and total timeout are bounded, (d) jitter is applied to avoid thundering herd."
    },
    {
      "severity": "medium",
      "title": "Six rounds of post-review hotfixes suggest residual instability",
      "body": "Commit log shows: 'Bundle C post-review hotfixes', 'Bundle D3+ post-review hotfixes', 'Bundle E+ simplify pass (4 P1 + 2 P2 cleanups)', 'Bundle F — ReferenceError + full thread/resume scaffolding purge', 'fix(v0.4.3 hotfixes): 5 bugs from 3-agent full-project review', 'fix(v0.4.2): sync codex-plugin-cc', 'fix(v0.4.1): trim'. A pattern of P1 hotfixes after each bundle suggests incomplete pre-merge testing. The latest change (v0.4.6, e0a9ba8) adds BigModel error dispatch + retry — a new subsystem following the same pattern.",
      "file": "CHANGELOG.md",
      "line_start": 1,
      "line_end": 521,
      "confidence": 0.7,
      "recommendation": "Before tagging v0.4.7, run the full UAT suite (test-automation/uat-reports/) against the new retry/backoff paths and publish the report. Verify that the v0.4.5 UAT scenarios (which include wait/background modes) still pass after the v0.4.6 error-dispatch changes."
    },
    {
      "severity": "medium",
      "title": "state.mjs + tracked-jobs.mjs modifications — race condition surface in concurrent job management",
      "body": "Both state.mjs (+32 lines changed) and tracked-jobs.mjs (+78 lines) were modified. These manage job lifecycle state. The diff adds --wait/--background execution modes (d573807) and modifies job control. File-based state with no locking (typical for ESM CLI tools) is vulnerable to TOCTOU races when multiple GLM invocations run concurrently on the same project. Without seeing the implementation, the risk is that two concurrent invocations could corrupt shared state files.",
      "file": "scripts/lib/tracked-jobs.mjs",
      "line_start": 1,
      "line_end": 78,
      "confidence": 0.5,
      "recommendation": "Verify tracked-jobs.mjs uses atomic file writes (write-to-temp + rename) for state mutations, and that --wait polling reads stale-safe state. If two invocations can run simultaneously against the same .glm/ directory, add a file lock or pid-based guard."
    }
  ],
  "next_steps": [
    "Provide contents of scripts/lib/retry.mjs, scripts/lib/bigmodel-errors.mjs, and scripts/lib/glm-client.mjs for line-level adversarial review of the retry/backoff subsystem.",
    "Run `grep -rn 'process\\.mjs\\|/process[\"'\\''"]' scripts/` to confirm the deleted process.mjs has no remaining importers.",
    "Execute v0.4.5 UAT scenarios against current HEAD and publish results before tagging v0.4.7.",
    "Verify atomic writes in tracked-jobs.mjs and state.mjs to rule out concurrent-invocation state corruption."
  ]
}
